DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. SANCTIONS OF $1600 ARE GRANTED AGAINST DEFENDANT, PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE DATE OF ORDER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND E-MAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM JAMES FLEMING AT JAMESHFLEMING@GMAIL.COM. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY, PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Jul-18-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION's Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions

The motion to compel further production is granted in part and denied in part.

Defendant has attempted to justify redactions of signatory identity, wage rates and applicable groups from its contract document production on grounds of privacy.

Defendant did not object to production on grounds of privacy and that objection has been waived. CCP sec. 2031.240(b)(2) (objection in response for request for production must state "extent of, and the specific ground of objection"). "There is no provision for the subsequent filing of objections. . . . This court may not consider the objections to the several interrogatories which [the defendant] has raised for the first time in this proceeding." West Pico Furniture Co. v. Superior Court, 56 Cal.2d 407, 414 (1961).

Defendant did object on grounds of relevance, but the identity of signatories to the agreements is relevant as disclosing potential witnesses or sources of discoverable information. The contractual wage rates and categories, however, are not relevant to the subject matter and those redactions may remain.

Defendant shall produce a copies of each agreement produced in response to Request for Production No. 3 unredacted as to identification of signatories within 15 days of the date of order.

Given that the motion was granted in part and denied in part, sanctions of $1600 are granted against defendant, payable to plaintiff within 30 days of the date of order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem James Fleming at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-17-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint, as follows:

Defendant's motion to strike the punitive damages claim is denied.

Plaintiff alleges theories of agency and ratification because Defendant's employee sexually harassed and assaulted Plaintiff in the bathroom at Westfield Centre. The complaint pleads that Defendant's employee opened the bathroom stall door, watched Plaintiff, a dependent adult with physical and mental disabilities, as he was using the toilet, then minutes later "approached Plaintiff from behind, cranked up, and slammed Plaintiff in the back using his mop as a weapon," and thereafter, Defendant released it employee after little inquiry and subsequently refused Plaintiff's request for critical information and security footage. "Malice" means either (1) conduct that is intended by the defendant to cause injury to the plaintiff or (2) despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (See Civil Code Section 3294(c)(1).) Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to satisfy both elements. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts re ratification, as well. (See Civil Code Section 3294(b).) Plaintiff's intentional tort claim may sound under the Elder Abuse Act and he has pled facts sufficient to claim punitive damages under the Act. (See Welf. & Inst. Code Sections 15610.23, 15610.63 and 15657; Conservatorship of Kayle (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 1, 6.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/JMQ)
